mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, lifting S&P 500 valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off equity repricing within one month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based demand expansion, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60 percent of observations across a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains elevated above 315 while policy rates stay restrictive, real rates stay high, compressing equity multiples and depressing forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly lower when both series exceed thresholds simultaneously compared to baseline periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and healthy aggregate supply, reducing input cost pressures and supporting near-term equity upside.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year Spearman correlation between INDPRO changes and 5-day SPX returns is negative across all test windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent signals a hardening recession, which systematically reduces forward earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of sub-1.5 percent growth episodes over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising employment levels paired with stable or falling unemployment confirm a strengthening labor market without overheating, boosting consumer spending expectations and lifting equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive forward returns in at least 50 percent of instances over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales exceed 700000 while housing starts remain robust, durable consumer demand is confirmed, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Forward returns are flat or negative when both series exceed thresholds simultaneously, breaking the earnings revision link.
